item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thought this was going to take a dark turn but was pleasantly surprised. +1 Great writing.

item.title: Belated +100
item.content:encoded:
Noting that these adorably pathetic things have surpassed +100 with 0 downvotes.

Tunnel progress: 1 cm 0.1 mm1

Footnotes
1. see maintenance ticket 2258-10: Manual re-fluffing of wall padding tile 74

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Easy solution. Let them "escape", into a disguised containment cell run by a "different agency" that they're not angry at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is written excellently. You can't help but love their tenacity despite being completely ineffectual. You did an excellent job of making it both adorable and slightly unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so creepy if you imagine a bunch of baloons talking to you while tied to your mailbox. I wonder if they were originally kids or if they're just a sentient object.

I love it when I get to watch an idea on the brainstorming thread turn into a real SCP.

Edit: Hey, I just noticed that SCP-2257 is listed right next to this. I wonder…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intelligent enough to know they exist, but with no way to make use of their intelligence.

Welcome to my world.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear lord I love this article that is one amazing first paragraph of description.

Also pretty much everything in the addenda is brilliant. Like this:

SCP-2258 forms itself into a very crude humanoid shape. SCP-2258 refers to itself as Dr. Kasrzyszak and requests to be let out of containment. Request denied.

Also the balloon trying to break the walls down via force. Someone protect it. Pls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this :) really fastastic SCP. Actually so far this is my favorite SCP.

item.title: Great Job, Here are some Critiques
item.content:encoded:
This is very innocent on the surface, but a bit sinister after the forty-one thirty-six, those poor balloons D:
I can only say you might need to clean up the incident logs and delineate the Singular vs. Plural on the pronouns, I did get caught up on that for a bit.
The image of a set of Balloons stacking together to form the crude image of a man is deliciously funny
All in all
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Shaggy. I've been having trouble with some of the formatting.

For those curious, this photograph was taken by Alan Cleaver, and is free to use. https://www.flickr.com/photos/alancleaver/4363025139

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +45 with no downvotes, congrats dude!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, these poor dumb things. :)

Only critique: it's 'amnestics'. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me happy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intelligent enough to know they exist, but with no way to make use of their intelligence. Funny on the surface but disturbing underneath, like candy from Herman Fuller's circus.
Sartre would have approved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So funny, but dark kind of dark at the same time. I liked it.

At the begining it made me laugh, closer to the end though I felt sorry for the ballons.

I also think that you could have explained under which circunstances the other ballons lost their sentience.

I think that this information: "Until recent events (see Incident Log-2258)", could be better integrated to the description. Maybe it should be all in brackets, or replaced with a line at the end of the paragraph explaining that now the ballons don't behave the same way anymore, then add "(see Incident Log-2258)" at the end.

In the fourth paragraph of the description you wrote "tried", instead of "tired".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw there was a file uploaded, deleted, and reuploaded. I took this to mean you were having trouble getting the image block to work. I added the picture. If you actually did not want the image, simply revert. If you keep it, don't forget to edit the caption if you feel it's necessary, at the moment it is a generic statement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is hilarious in a lightheartedly dark way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only minor complaint I have is that I would've liked to see mentioned what happened to the five "dead" balloons. Presumably mishaps during the experiments, probably at least one even on purpose until they found out that they don't regain their properties.

This is a really minor thing though and the rest of the article is absolutely adorable. I love that stuff like this also has a place on a site that is usually dedicated to horror-like stuff. Happily upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is horribly depressing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I forgot to thank you. Thanks for the critique you provided by private message!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:) this is good and pure and is a shining example of all things right in the world

Have your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could upvote twice, I would. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anything that makes me contemplate the concept of "helium dependency" is typically worth an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is why I hate collective nouns…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic, and is 100% genuinely adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have liked to see what these balloons sound like when they're despondent, but beyond that, I'm really into this. It's Fun with a capital F.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, it's absolutely adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you read the Incident Logs, you'll notice that ALL of SCP-2258 is participating in the escape, not just David. So obviously they all feel the same way. I'm glad you like the escape attempts!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding dredlocks, this is incredibly cute

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first SCP! Hope you guys like it! Huge thanks to (in no particular order) Scorpion451, Petrograd, psul, Comparing Realities, Decibelles, Communism will win, and everyone else who commented on the drafts in the forums.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I do enjoy the extent (and futility) of David the balloon's escape attempts, the SCP is multiple balloons. What about the rest of the balloons? This feels to be a big plot hole. If the article focused on that one balloon from the get-go, it would be a non-issue. But do other sentient balloons feel the same way? Do they empathise their friend? Do they help David? Do they hate David?

Thus, I think the other balloons should also be accounted for in the article beyond broad mentions and definitions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved this as a draft, love it here. Cute, and charming as hell. +1.
